---
title: "Cutner-Nina_Intermediate-elective-02"
author: "Nina Cutner"
format: pdf
date: May 12, 2026
execute: 
  messages: false
  warning: false
---

# Set up

## Packages

```{r}
#| label: Packages

# data visualization
library(tidyverse)

# cleaning column names
library(janitor)

# working with file paths
library(here)

# working with dates
library(lubridate)

# improved text rendering in ggplot
library(ggtext)

# direct labeling for plots
library(ggrepel)
```

## Data

```{r}
#| label: Data

# read in vegetation data
veg <- read_csv(here("data", "veg copy.csv"))
```
# 1. Explaining inspiration

The visualization I chose for my inspiration is Steven Ponce's, "Coal falls. Renewables rise."

This visualization style works well for the NCOS vegetation dataset because the project focuses on ecological change through time, specifically shifts in native vegetation cover between 2017 and 2025. Similar to how Ponce's figure highlights transitions in energy systems, this visualization highlights changes in vegetation communities at NCOS.

The visualization will display year on the x-axis and percent cover on the y-axis. Separate lines will represent the dominant native plant species observed at NCOS between 2017 and 2025. Color will be used to distinguish species, and direct labels at the ends of lines will identify each species rather than using a legend. This design follows the style of the original visualization by emphasizing long-term ecological change through smooth, flowing trends over time.

# 2. Planning the figure

![Initial sketch of final visualization](../figures/sketch.jpg)
# 3. Coding the figure

```{r}
#| label: clean-data

# vector of selected native species
native_species <- c(
  "Salicornia_pacifica",
  "Distichlis_spicata",
  "Jaumea_carnosa",
  "Stipa_pulchra",
  "Frankenia_salina"
)

# create cleaned vegetation object
veg_clean <- veg |>
  # clean column names
  clean_names()

# filter to selected native species
veg_native <- veg_clean |>
  filter(psoc %in% native_species)

```

```{r}
#| label: native-vegetation-figure
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

# summarize selected native species by year
veg_summary <- veg_native |> 
  group_by(year, psoc) |> 
  summarize(
    mean_cover = mean(percent_cover, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |> 
  mutate(
    species_label = str_replace_all(psoc, "_"," ")
  )
# create data frame for direct labels at the final year
veg_labels <- veg_summary |> 
  group_by(species_label) |> 
  filter(year == max(year)) |> 
  ungroup()

# create Ponce-inspired native vegetation trend figure
native_veg_plot <- ggplot(
  data = veg_summary,
  
  # map variables to plot aesthetics
  mapping = aes(
    
    # map year to x-axis
    x = year,
    
    # map mean percent cover to y-axis
    y = mean_cover,
    
    # color lines by species
    color = species_label,
    
    # group observations by species
    group = species_label
  )
) +
  
  # add trend lines for each species
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.5) +
  
  # add points for yearly observations
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  
  # directly label species at end of lines
  geom_text_repel(
    data = veg_labels,
    mapping = aes(label = species_label),
    
    # shift labels slightly right of final year
    nudge_x = 0.4,
    
    # keep labels vertically organized
    direction = "y",
    
    # align labels to left side
    hjust = 0,
    
    # remove connecting segments
    segment.color = NA,
    
    # label text size
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  
  # customize x-axis scale and limits
  scale_x_continuous(
    
    # display yearly breaks
    breaks = seq(2021, 2025, by = 1),
    
    # extend axis slightly for labels
    limits = c(2021, 2026)
  ) +
  
  # customize y-axis scale and spacing
  scale_y_continuous(
    
    # set y-axis limits
    limits = c(0, 65),
    
    # add slight space above lines
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))
  ) +
  
  # manually assign colors to species
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Salicornia pacifica" = "#29B6F6",
      "Distichlis spicata" = "#FF6F61",
      "Jaumea carnosa" = "#F4B400",
      "Stipa pulchra" = "#D16BE0",
      "Frankenia salina" = "#2ECC71"
    )
  ) +
  
  # add plot title and axis labels
  labs(
    title = "Native vegetation changes through time at NCOS",
    subtitle = "Mean percent cover of five dominant native plant species",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Mean percent cover"
  ) +
  
  # apply minimal base theme
  theme_minimal() +
  
  # customize dark Ponce-inspired appearance
  theme(
    
    # remove legend
    legend.position = "none",
    
    # set dark panel background
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#111111", color = NA),
    
    # set dark plot background
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#111111", color = NA),
    
    # remove minor grid lines
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    
    # customize major grid lines
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#2B2B2B"),
    
    # customize title appearance
    plot.title = element_text(
      face = "bold",
      size = 20,
      color = "white"
    ),
    
    # customize subtitle appearance
    plot.subtitle = element_text(
      size = 11,
      color = "gray80"
    ),
    
    # customize y-axis title
    axis.title.y = element_text(
      size = 11,
      color = "white"
    ),
    
    # customize axis text
    axis.text = element_text(
      size = 10,
      color = "white"
    ),
    
    # remove x-axis title
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    
    # remove axis lines
    axis.line = element_blank(),
    
    # set all plot text to white
    text = element_text(color = "white")
  )

# display plot
native_veg_plot
```
```{r}
#| label: save-figure

# save final figure to figures folder
ggsave(
  filename = here("figures", "native-vegetation-trends.png"),
  
  # figure to save
  plot = native_veg_plot,
  
  # output size
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  
  # image resolution
  dpi = 300
)
```
